items.subject: Security Vulnerability Report — Exposure of Executive Email Inbox and Employee PII via Public GitHub Repository
items.body_preview:
You don't often get email from kernick24@proton.me. Learn why this is important
Hi Galaxy Data Centers Team,

I identified a critical data exposure affecting Galaxy Data Centers, Galaxy Capital Partners, and Redhill Data Centre that I'd like to report
items.ai_summary: A security researcher (kernick24@proton.me) has discovered a critical data exposure involving Galaxy's executive emails, employee PII, and potentially sensitive information about Redhill Data Centre exposed on a public GitHub repository. This is a legitimate security vulnerability disclosure that requires immediate investigation and remediation.
items.ai_action: Dave must see this immediately. Forward to: (1) Galaxy's CTO/security lead, (2) Legal/compliance, (3) Castleforge Partners (co-investor in Redhill), (4) SMBC/ICG (lenders). Kernick appears to be conducting responsible disclosure. Respond promptly to prevent escalation.
ai_next_steps: Dave reviews the full email and any attached details from Kernick immediately
Designate security/legal lead to contact Kernick within 24 hours to understand scope and verify their claims
Conduct internal audit of GitHub repos, S3 buckets, and public code repositories for exposed credentials/data
Notify insurance broker and consider breach counsel if data exposure is confirmed
Brief Castleforge Partners and lenders as required by partnership/lending agreements

The Moderator for this session will be Randy Borron, Vice Chairman at Cushma
items.ai_summary: Matthew Welch from Data Center Nation is organizing a prep call for the DCN Toronto event scheduled for June 9, 2026, with Randy Borron (Vice Chairman at Cushman & Wakefield) moderating a session on Canada as a data center investment opportunity. This is a strategic industry conference focused on Canadian data center growth and global investment trends, representing a key networking and thought leadership opportunity for Galaxy Capital Partners.
ai_next_steps: Confirm Dave's availability for the prep call and determine which Galaxy executives should participate

items.subject: Financial Services Deck V1
items.body_preview:
Hey Team,
I'm sharing the first draft of the financial services deck I've prepared. PFA. ​Galaxy_Redhill_FS_May.pptx​

London has many colocation providers. This deck positions Galaxy Redhill against that field on one major axis - regulated FS workload
items.ai_summary: SA has completed the initial version of a competitive positioning deck for Galaxy Redhill's financial services offering, focusing on the regulated FS workload segment. This deck appears designed to differentiate Galaxy against London's crowded colocation market by emphasizing compliance and regulatory capabilities. The positioning matters because capturing regulated FS workloads typically commands premium margins and long-term customer commitments.
ai_next_steps: Review the deck for messaging clarity, competitive claims verification, and alignment with Galaxy's actual FS compliance certifications and capabilities
Identify any gaps in our FS service offerings or certifications versus competitors highlighted in the deck
Schedule feedback session with SA and France to refine messaging before using deck for prospect meetings or investor communications

items.subject: Fw: Possible Unit 3 sales capacity
items.body_preview:
Dave

Not saying that there is no capacity at Unit 3.
However, the capacity is subjective - and as can be seen by what Nomura is asking for in their TDD, a position needs to be established as questions such as those in cells E21, E34 & E47 - influenced
items.ai_summary: Rhys Jones is flagging that while Unit 3 may have available capacity, the definition and measurement of that capacity is unclear and needs to be formalized. Nomura's technical due diligence request has exposed gaps in how Galaxy currently quantifies and documents capacity metrics, which could impact their ability to close deals and provide credible capacity statements to prospects.
ai_next_steps: Schedule a meeting with Rhys and the operations team to standardize capacity measurement definitions and establish clear documentation protocols for Unit 3
Review Nomura's TDD questions (specifically cells E21, E34, E47) to understand what capacity data they require and map current Galaxy systems to those requirements
Develop a capacity position paper or technical specification that addresses standardized metrics before responding to future customer due diligence requests

items.ai_summary: Rhys Jones from Galaxy Data Centers is requesting clarification on UPS operational procedures and documentation, specifically asking for standard startup, shutdown, and bypass transfer protocols. This appears to be an internal request to resolve a gap in UPS operational documentation or procedures that should be standardized across the organization. Clear UPS procedures are critical for Galaxy's data center operations to ensure reliability, minimize downtime risk, and maintain service level agreements with customers.

Schedule a brief call between Rhys, France, and the facilities/operations team to confirm these procedures align with Galaxy's actual equipment and deployment standards
